title: Semantic Versioning
content:
I've been involved in projects for about 10 years, covering a considerable number of projects. However, there's no unified standard for version management. A while ago, a colleague recommended Semantic Versioning to me, which provides a standardized definition for software version numbers. In reality, each company has its own way of defining software version numbers, so they generally differ.

Here's a brief explanation of this concept.

Version Format:`主版本号.次版本号.修订号`The version number increment rules are as follows:

* Major version number: When you make incompatible API changes.

* Minor version number: When you make backward-compatible feature additions.

* Revision number: When you make backward-compatible bug fixes.

Previous version numbers and version build metadata can be added after "major version number.minor version number.revision number" as extensions.

> For detailed explanations and instructions, please visit the official website [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org)
